2604.02986.title: Mitigating Reward Hacking in RLHF via Advantage Sign Robustness
2604.02986.paper_summary: Reward models (RMs) used in reinforcement learning from human feedback (RLHF) are vulnerable to reward hacking: as the policy maximizes a learned proxy reward, true quality plateaus or degrades. We make the assumption that reward hacking is often caused by flipped advantage signs: instead of reducing the likelihood of a bad response, a flipped sign causes the update to increase it. By considering an adversarial perturbation in the RM parameter space, we can derive a certified sign-preservation radius, which is the smallest perturbation that can flip the advantage sign during policy optimization. Based on this formulation, we propose Sign-Certified Policy Optimization (SignCert-PO), down-weighting non-robust completions in the policy gradient update. Unlike prior approaches that require multiple RMs or access to the RM training data, SignCert-PO is lightweight and operates purely at the policy optimization stage using only the RM parameters and on-policy completions. On TL;DR summarization and AlpacaFarm benchmarks, SignCert-PO consistently achieves a better win rate than baselines and reduces reward hacking.
2604.02986.paper_summary_zh: 獎勵模型（RMs）在從人類反饋的強化學習（RLHF）中易受到獎勵黑客攻擊：當策略最大化學習到的代理獎勵時，真實質量會停滯或下降。 我們假設獎勵黑客攻擊通常是由於優勢符號翻轉造成的：翻轉的符號不僅沒有減少不良反應的可能性，反而會使更新增加它。 通過考慮RM參數空間中的對抗性擾動，我們可以推導出一個經過認證的符號保持半徑，這是可以在策略優化過程中翻轉優勢符號的最小擾動。 基於這一公式，我們提出了符號認證策略優化（SignCert-PO），在策略梯度更新中降低非穩健完成的權重。 與以往需要多個RMs或訪問RM訓練數據的方法不同，SignCert-PO是輕量級的，僅在策略優化階段運作，僅使用RM參數和在政策上完成的結果。 在TL;DR摘要和AlpacaFarm基準測試中，SignCert-PO始終比基線獲得更好的勝率並減少獎勵黑客攻擊。
2604.02986.author: Shinnosuke Ono et.al.
2604.02986.authors: Shinnosuke Ono, Johannes Ackermann, Soichiro Nishimori, Takashi Ishida, Masashi Sugiyama
